cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this problem step by step. So the question is asking between what times David could have gone to the football field. The key here is to figure out when David wasn't doing other things, right? Because if he was seen doing other activities during certain times, he couldn't have been at the football field then. First, let me list out all the given information: 1. David woke up at 5am. So that's when he started his day. 2. Steven saw David buying a phone from 5am to 6am. So between 5 and 6, he's at the electronics store. 3. Kimberly saw him taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 6am to 8am. So 6 to 8, he's in Pisa? Interesting. 4. Then Betty saw him working out at the gym from 9am to 12pm. So 9 to 12, gym time. 5. Lisa saw him driving to the water park from 12pm to 3pm. So 12 to 3, he's on the road to the water park. 6. William saw David sitting on a rooftop from 3pm to 10pm. So from 3 until 10, he's on the rooftop. 7. Also, the football field was closed after 10pm. So David must have gone before that. Now, the question is, when could he have gone to the football field? So we need to find the time intervals where he wasn't doing any of the above activities and the football field is open. Let me map out the timeline: - 5am: Wakes up. - 5am-6am: At electronics store (Steven). - 6am-8am: At Leaning Tower of Pisa (Kimberly). - 8am-9am: What's happening here? There's a gap between 8am and 9am. Because after Kimberly sees him until 8am, the next activity is at 9am at the gym. So between 8am and 9am, there's no mentioned activity. - 9am-12pm: Gym (Betty). - 12pm-3pm: Driving to water park (Lisa). - 3pm-10pm: On rooftop (William). - After
